rulings.Case Title: ANNE W. THAYER VS. TRINITY PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Specially Prepared Interrogatorires
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 7, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS ANNE THAYER and ERNEST THAYER'S Motion To Compel Answers To Specially Prepared Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. It would be helpful to the court if the parties were prepared to discuss their positions on whether or not the interrogatories seek information necessary for a class certification motion, and the extent to which the discovery to-date is sufficient to enable plaintiff to make a motion for class certifications. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN H. DANNER III et al VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Donald Durkee, Stephen Engler, Richard Cotton, Raycardo Aviles Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Lesser Sanctions, Against Defendant For Their Intentional Spoliation Of Relevant Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 7, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS DONALD DURKEE, STEPHEN ENGLER, RICHARD COTTON and RAYCARDO AVILES' Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Lesser Sanctions, Against Defendant For Their Intentional Spoliation Of Relevant Evidence. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. It would be helpful to the court if the parties were prepared to address their positions on the following issues: (1) whether the information in the destroyed scoring keys could be used to prove or disprove a necessary element of the first cause of action for age discrimination in plaintiffs' fourth amended complaint, and if so, how; (2) what other information already produced during discovery could be used to prove or disprove any such element of plaintiffs' first cause of action; (3) when the duty to preserve material related to the 2008 H-20 Leutenant Examination arose; and (4) what evidence, if any, supports an inference that the destruction of the scoring keys was the result of anything more than mere negligence. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RUBEN MALDONADO, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL et al VS. ME CAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 7, 2013, Line 27, DEFENDANT ME CAL, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary adjudication is denied. As to the 3rd cause of action, there are triable issues of material fact whether Plaintiff has a meal period policy and whether Plaintiff Loiza was provided a 30 minute meal period during which he was relieved of all duties. (See evidence cited by Plaintiff in response to Defendant's UMF 2 (Loiza Decl. 3-7); UMF 4 (Loiza Decl. 3-7, Maldonado Decl. 5-6); UMF 5 (Loiza Decl. 3-7; Maldonado Decl. 5-6).) See also evidence cited in support of Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts 6, 8, 9, 11. As to the 4th cause of action, there are triable issues of material fact whether Plaintiff had a rest break policy and whether plaintiff was provided rest breaks required by law. (See evidence cited by Plaintiff in response to UMF 9 (Loiza Decl. 12-14); UMF 10 (same evidence); UMF 11 (Loiza Decl. 13). See also evidence cited in support of Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts 23, 24. Ruling on evidentiary objections: Defendant's objections are all overruled. Plaintiff's objections are all overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: FIRST PROVIDEN LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Approval Of Trasnfer Of Structured Settlemnt Payment Rights;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 7, 2013, Line 10, PETITIONER FIRST PROVIDEN LLC Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Approval Of Trasnfer Of Structured Settlemnt Payment Rights; Hearing required. First Providen should be prepared to discuss whether it is in the best interest of the transferor Ms. Quitania for the Court to approve the requested transfer of structured settlement rights (Ins. Code § 10139.5(a)(1)) in light of annuity issuer Berkshire Hathaway Life Insurance Company of Nebraska's ("BHLN") response to the same petition filed in related case no. CPF-12-512609 that if BHLN accelerated the remaining guaranteed payments of the annuity, the result would be a lump sum payment of $ 80,593.74, which is greater than the amount of $ 52,662.61 offered by First Providen. BHLN should be prepared to discuss the proper procedure for a Court order accelerating payments. First Providen is requested to state whether it served Ms. Quitania in San Francisco with a copy of BHLN's response and declaration to the petition in the related case. First Providen is also requested to provide notice of the information set forth herein on the Court's website, including that a hearing is required, by email and telephone to Ms. Quintania and BHLN's counsel no later than February 6, 2013 at 4:00 pm. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: 209 POST STREET, L.P. VS. VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY III LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Charging Partner'S Inerest In Limited Partnership
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 7, 2013, Line 11, PLAINTIFF 209 POST STREET, L.P. Notice Of Motion For Order Charging Partner'S Inerest In Limited Partnership Denied without prejudice. Plaintiff must establish that it has served all of the partners of Venture Strategy Partners II LP. (CCP § 708.320(a). Although Plaintiff has served a number of third parties and the Proof of Service states at the top "(All Partners of Venture Strategy Partners II, L.P.)," Plaintiff has not presented any evidence to establish that these in fact are all of the partners of Venture Strategy Partners II, L.P. rulings.Case Title: FLORENCE LEWIS VS. ERIC TABAS MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 7, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT JENTA SHEN MD MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion is denied because defendant fails to shift the burden. Plaintiff's claim against Dr. Shen was not limited to the surgery. The Complaint alleges that defendants negligently performed the surgery and the treatment. "Surgery" and "treatment" are each distinctly pled. See, e.g., Para. 21. ("[t]hat the treatment and surgery performed by Defendants, and each of them. . . negligently failed to conform to the standard of care both with respect to the care and treatment rendered to plaintiff, and with respect to providing to Plaintiff information about the risks and hazards, or other harmful consequences, that might follow from the treatment, diagnosis or surgery the Defendants, and each of them, planned and performed for Plaintiff.") Further, the Complaint states that "such failure to remove all or part of the cervix, and other acts and omissions by the Defendants, constitute a breach of the applicable standard of care." Pursuant to CCP 452, the complaint pleads a theory of liability based upon the related treatment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MATT BLACK MR VS. HIGHTOWER PRODUCTION LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 7, 2013, Line 26, DEFENDANT HIGHTOWER PRODUCTION LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The demurrer is overruled. The complaint is sufficiently pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
